titleInfo.title: Dharma Bell and Dhāraṇī Pillar: Li Bo’s Buddhist Inscriptions

mods.abstract: Kroll, p. 40–46, relates everything known about the coming into being of the five Chinese translations (T.967–T.971) of the Uṣṇīṣavijayādhāraṇī. On p. 46–48, he gives a summary of the sutra’s content. On pp. 49–75, he studies the panegyric inscription Li Bo 李白 (701–762?) composed for the dhāraṇī pillar of Chongming Monastery 崇明寺 in Lujun 魯郡 (present Yanzhou 兗州 in Shandong). [note by CW: the governmental seat of Lujun was in Xiaqiu 瑕丘 (also written Shaqiu 沙丘) county town, this is the place where the "Eastern Shaqiu Nunnery" was located, from which a pedestal with an inscription attributed to Seng’an Daoyi stems, see Jung_Lu2006_07).] Chongming Si was probably also located in Xiaqiu. The dhāraṇī pillar originally stood in the market place of Lujun. After the vinaya master Daozong 道宗 of Chongming Si died on May 21, 749, it was decided to move the dhāraṇī pillar from the market place to the monastery, probably in honor of Daozong. For this occasion, Li Bo was asked to contribute a text for the re-erection of the pillar.

name.namePart: Scuola Italiana di Studi sull’Asia Orientale

titleInfo.title: Italian School of East Asian Studies. Epigraphical Series
